# Gumroad Sales Page Template

**Asset 4 of 5 — Sell the Client Docs You Already Use**

---

## Purpose

Build your complete Gumroad product listing using fill-in-the-blank sections.
Copy each block directly into Gumroad's product description editor.

**Time required:** 30–45 minutes
**What you need:** Your completed Offer Copy Card from Asset 3

---

## How to Use This Template

1. Open your Gumroad product editor
2. Work through each section below
3. Replace every [BRACKET] with your own content
4. Copy the formatted text into Gumroad's description field
5. Preview before publishing

**Note:** Gumroad's description editor supports basic formatting.
Use line breaks and simple headers — avoid complex markdown.

---

## SECTION 1 — Product Title

This is the name shown on your Gumroad listing and in search results.

**Format:** Keep it under 60 characters. Lead with the outcome.

**Template:**
```
[Outcome] [Document Type] for [Buyer]
```

**Examples:**
- The Revision-Free Creative Brief for Freelance Copywriters
- Client Intake Template for Copywriters Who Want Fewer Revision Rounds
- Brand Voice Messaging Framework for Solo Marketers

**Your product title:**
___________________________________

---

## SECTION 2 — Product Subtitle (Summary Line)

This appears under your title in the Gumroad product card.
One sentence. Under 120 characters.

**Template:**
```
A [FORMAT] that helps [BUYER] [DO WHAT] without [PAIN].
```

**Examples:**
- A 30-question intake form that helps freelance copywriters get full project
  clarity before starting a draft — without chasing clients for answers.
- A structured messaging framework that helps solo marketers define brand voice
  in one session — without three rounds of back-and-forth.

**Your subtitle:**
___________________________________

---

## SECTION 3 — Product Description

Copy the blocks below into Gumroad's description editor in order.
Replace all [BRACKETS].

---

### BLOCK 1 — Opening Hook (3–5 lines)

```
[Relatable frustration or situation in 1 sentence.]

[Name the specific problem. Be concrete.]

[Name the outcome your template creates. One sentence.]

That's what this template is for.
```

**Example:**
```
Most copywriting projects don't go sideways during the writing.
They go sideways during the briefing.

Vague answers, missing information, unclear expectations —
and you end up doing two extra revision rounds that weren't in the contract.

This intake template fixes that before the project starts.

That's what this template is for.
```

**Your opening hook:**
___________________________________
___________________________________
___________________________________

---

### BLOCK 2 — What It Is

```
── ABOUT THIS TEMPLATE ──

[Product name] is a [format] for [buyer description].

[What it does — 2 sentences. Practical, specific.]

[Where it fits in their work process — 1 sentence.]
```

**Example:**
```
── ABOUT THIS TEMPLATE ──

The Copywriter's Client Intake Form is a 30-question briefing document
for freelance copywriters.

It captures everything you need before you write a word:
project scope, target audience, tone, deadlines, budget,
and the information clients always forget to tell you.

Use it in your first client call, or send it as a pre-call form
before the contract is signed.
```

**Your "About This Template" block:**
___________________________________
___________________________________
___________________________________

---

### BLOCK 3 — What You'll Get

```
── WHAT YOU'LL GET ──

• [Item 1 — specific and concrete]
• [Item 2]
• [Item 3]
• [Item 4]
• [Item 5 — if applicable]
```

**Example:**
```
── WHAT YOU'LL GET ──

• A 30-question intake form organized by category
  (project basics, audience, tone, deliverables, logistics)
• Instructions for every question — when to ask it,
  what a useful answer looks like, and what to do if the client isn't sure
• An editable Google Doc you can customize to your niche or service type
• 3 AI prompts to adapt the questions for your specific type of work
• PDF version for quick reference
```

**Your "What You'll Get" block:**
___________________________________
___________________________________
___________________________________

---

### BLOCK 4 — For You If

```
── THIS IS FOR YOU IF ──

• [Specific situation — buyer characteristic 1]
• [Specific situation — buyer characteristic 2]
• [Specific frustration they recognize in themselves]
• [Specific goal they have]
• [One more relevant situation]
```

**Example:**
```
── THIS IS FOR YOU IF ──

• You're a freelance copywriter with at least 3 clients under your belt
• Your current intake process is scattered across email, Slack, and verbal notes
• You've had a project go sideways because the brief was unclear
• You want a more professional, structured start to every project
• You're tired of asking follow-up questions mid-draft
```

**Your "For You If" block:**
___________________________________
___________________________________
___________________________________

---

### BLOCK 5 — Not For You If (Optional)

```
── THIS IS NOT FOR YOU IF ──

• [Who this won't help — honest and specific]
• [Another situation where this isn't the right fit]
• [One more if needed]
```

**Example:**
```
── THIS IS NOT FOR YOU IF ──

• You're looking for a template you can use without any customization
• You haven't started client work yet (this helps you serve clients better,
  not get them)
• You need a sales page or email writing service
```

**Your "Not For You If" block:**
___________________________________
___________________________________
___________________________________

---

### BLOCK 6 — Before / After

```
── BEFORE AND AFTER ──

BEFORE: [The situation they're in now — specific, not generic]

AFTER: [The improvement your template creates — concrete outcome]
```

**Example:**
```
── BEFORE AND AFTER ──

BEFORE: You finish the first call with half the information you need.
You send two follow-up emails. The client sends vague answers.
You start writing anyway and end up with three revision rounds.

AFTER: You send the intake form before the call.
The client fills it in. You show up knowing exactly what to write.
The project runs on your terms.
```

**Your "Before and After" block:**
___________________________________
___________________________________
___________________________________

---

### BLOCK 7 — Closing and CTA

```
── READY TO USE IT? ──

[One sentence summarizing the core value — practical, direct.]

[Price] — instant download. Editable Google Doc + PDF.

Questions? Email [{{SUPPORT_EMAIL}}]
I respond to every message.
```

**Example:**
```
── READY TO USE IT? ──

If you've built a briefing process that works,
this is how you make it consistent every time.

$19 — instant download. Editable Google Doc + PDF.

Questions? Email {{SUPPORT_EMAIL}}
I respond to every message.
```

**Your closing block:**
___________________________________
___________________________________

---

## SECTION 4 — Gumroad Settings Checklist

Before publishing, confirm these settings in your Gumroad dashboard:

**Pricing:**
☐ Price set to $19 (or your chosen launch price)
☐ "Name a fair price" turned OFF (fixed price)
☐ Refund policy: 30-day no-questions-asked (recommended — increases trust)

**Files:**
☐ Primary product file uploaded (PDF or Google Doc link in a PDF)
☐ File opens correctly when downloaded
☐ Filename is clean and professional (no "v2_FINAL_USE_THIS.pdf" naming)

**Product page:**
☐ Product title entered
☐ Description text pasted and formatted
☐ Cover image uploaded (recommended: plain text on colored background)
☐ No placeholder text remaining ([BRACKETS] all replaced)
☐ Preview looks correct in the Gumroad editor

**Email and delivery:**
☐ Post-purchase email set up (or using Gumroad's default)
☐ Confirmation email includes the download link
☐ You tested the purchase flow yourself (buy your own product at $0 to test)

**Publishing:**
☐ Product set to "Published"
☐ You copied the product URL to use in emails and posts

---

## SECTION 5 — Cover Image Text (for Canva or plain text)

If you're creating a simple cover image, use this text layout:

```
Line 1 (large): [Your product name]
Line 2 (medium): [Your subtitle — 1 short line]
Line 3 (small): [Format] · [Price]
```

**Example:**
```
Line 1: The Copywriter's Client Intake Form
Line 2: 30 questions. Full project clarity. Fewer revisions.
Line 3: Google Doc Template · $19
```

**Recommended specs:**
- Size: 1280 × 720px (Gumroad recommended)
- Background: solid color (navy, dark green, charcoal) + white text
- No stock photos needed

**Your cover image text:**
Line 1: ___________________________________
Line 2: ___________________________________
Line 3: ___________________________________

---

## SECTION 6 — Post-Purchase Message

This message appears on the Gumroad thank-you page after purchase.
Keep it short — the buyer wants to access their product, not read more copy.

**Template:**
```
Thank you for buying [product name].

Your download is attached above — click to download or save.

If you have questions while using the template, email {{SUPPORT_EMAIL}}.
I respond to every message.

Next step: open the [first file name] and follow the instructions.
You'll have what you need in under 30 minutes.
```

**Your post-purchase message:**
___________________________________
___________________________________
___________________________________

---

## AI Prompt: Review Your Gumroad Page Before Publishing

Paste into Claude or ChatGPT with your draft description:

```
I've written a Gumroad product page for a paid template.
Here is the full description:

[paste your complete Gumroad description]

Please review it for:
1. Clarity — is it immediately obvious who this is for and what they get?
2. Specificity — are there any vague claims I should make more concrete?
3. Trust — does the "not for you if" section feel honest?
4. Conversion — is there anything missing that a buyer would want to know
   before purchasing?
5. Length — is any section too long or should anything be cut?

Give me 3 specific suggestions to improve the page before I publish.
```

---

Your Gumroad product page is ready.
